chat.author: Vishnu Beeram
chat.text:
draft-stone-spring-mpte-sr describes how to realize MPTE (as discussed in the MPTE architecture document [draft-kompella-teas-mpte]) in Segment Routing networks using existing SR building blocks: SR Policy and Binding SID at each junction that makes up the DAG, managed by a centralized controller. Each transit junction resolves a BSID to invoke a per-junction SR Policy. As a co-author, I believe this document is ready for adoption (in SPRING, of course).
That said, I would also like to draw your attention to draft-ajp-spring-srv6-mpte — this takes a different approach (that works with or without a controller) for SRv6 networks. Instead of per-junction Binding SIDs and SR Policies, each MPTED tunnel uses a unique DAG Locator and DAG SID as the SRv6 forwarding context. Transit junctions perform plain IPv6 forwarding on the DAG Locator prefix — no Endpoint Behavior execution, no Binding SIDs, no per-junction SR Policies needed. We'd appreciate a read and feedback on the SPRING list.

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: @Pavan - thanks for pointing out that draft-ajp-spring-srv6-mpte!

chat.author: Vishnu Beeram
chat.text: Please take the comments for the next two 00 presentations to the list..
